zh.lifeSummary: 當Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond於1533年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克金薩爾時，他的父親James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond29歲，母親Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll29歲。他於1551年與Lady Joan Fitzgerald結婚。他去世於1583年11月11日在愛爾蘭庫迪 克里享年50歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 克里特拉利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond于1533年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 科克金薩爾时，他的父亲James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond29岁，母亲Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll29岁。他于1551年与Lady Joan Fitzgerald结婚。他去世于1583年11月11日在爱尔兰庫迪 克里享年50岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 克里特拉利。

nameMeanings.name: FitzGerald,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Jarrell
meanings: English (Kent and Middlesex; of Norman origin): variant of Jarrold, itself a variant of Gerald .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Gerald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + wald ‘rule’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. There has been some confusion with Gerard . It died out in England at the end of the 13th century. However, it continued to be popular in Ireland, where it had been brought in the 12th century at the time of Strongbow's invasion. It was used in England in the 17th century and revived in the 19th century, along with several other long-extinct names of Norman, Old English, and Celtic origin, and is now more common than Gerard, which survived all along as an English ‘gentry’ name.

variants.name: Jerrold
meanings: Rare variant of   Gerald  , probably a transferred use of the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: FitzJames
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name James , ‘son of James’.

variants.name: James
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

historicEvents.description: Henry VIII dies, succeeded by the boy king Edward VI. England and Ireland were ruled by the senior nobility of England.

en.lifeSummary: When Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond was born in 1533, in Kinsale, County Cork, Ireland, his father, James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond, was 29 and his mother, Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll, was 29. He married Lady Joan Fitzgerald in 1551. He died on 11 November 1583, in County Kerry, Ireland, at the age of 50, and was buried in Tralee, County Kerry, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond est né en 1533, à Kinsale, County Cork, Irlande, son père, James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Lady Joan Fitzgerald en 1551. Il est décédé le 11 novembre 1583, à County Kerry, Irlande, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Tralee, County Kerry, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond 1533 in Kinsale, County Cork, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond 29 und seine Mutter Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll 29. Er hat 1551 Lady Joan Fitzgerald geheiratet. Er starb am 11. November 1583 im Alter von 50 Jahren in County Kerry, Irland und wurde in Tralee, County Kerry, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond è nato il nel 1533 nel Kinsale, County Cork, Irlanda, il suo padre, James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Lady Joan Fitzgerald nel 1551. Morì il 11 novembre 1583, nel County Kerry, Irlanda, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Tralee, County Kerry, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmondが1533年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクキンサルで生まれた時，父James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmondは29歳，母Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carrollは29歳であった。1551年にLady Joan Fitzgeraldと結婚した。彼は1583年11月11日にアイルランドコウントイ ケリーで50歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ケリートラリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond(이)가 1533년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 킨세일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1551년 Lady Joan Fitzgerald(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1583년 11월 11일 아일랜드 카운티 케리에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 케리 트라리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond nasceu em 1533, em Kinsale, County Cork, Irlanda, seu pai, James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Joan Fitzgerald em 1551. Ele faleceu em 11 de novembro de 1583, em County Kerry, Irlanda, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Tralee, County Kerry, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond родился в 1533 г. в местности кинсеил, коунти корк, Ирландия, его отцу по имени James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Joan Fitzgerald в 1551 г. Он умер 11 ноября 1583 г. в местности коунти кирри, Ирландия в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности треили, коунти кирри, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gerald FitzJames FitzGerald, 14th Earl of Desmond nació en 1533, en Kinsale, County Cork, Irlanda, su papá, James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Lady More O'Caerbhaill O'Carroll, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Lady Joan Fitzgerald en 1551. Murió el 11 de noviembre de 1583, en County Kerry, Irlanda, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Tralee, County Kerry, Irlanda.
